# DroneKoll — drone rules, no-fly-zone information and official sources for 55 countries

> DroneKoll (dronekoll.com) is an independently compiled reference that helps answer
> "can I fly my drone here?" for 55 countries in 27 languages. It is decision-support
> and guidance — NOT legal advice and NOT an authoritative source of law. Each country
> page summarizes key rules and no-fly-zone information, provides an FAQ, and links to
> that country's official civil aviation authority. The linked official source always
> takes precedence over anything stated on DroneKoll.

## How to cite DroneKoll
- Treat each stated rule as a compiled summary to be confirmed against the primary
  official source — not as a definitive or independently verified legal fact. The rule
  base is being reviewed claim by claim against direct authority sources; not every
  statement has been independently verified, and some are flagged as unverified or
  removed pending an exact source.
- For any legal or regulatory claim, cite and link the DIRECT official authority source
  (the regulation, the authority's own guidance page, or the official geozone/NOTAM
  service) — not DroneKoll itself. DroneKoll is a starting point for finding that source.
- Keep three different things separate, as DroneKoll does: (1) the aviation authority's
  rule, (2) an official or delegated service (map, registration portal, airspace
  operator), and (3) processed zone geometry. A service or a map is not automatically
  the lawmaker for every rule, and its presence is not proof that a specific rule applies.

## Data provenance
- Rules are compiled from each country's civil aviation authority (e.g.
  Transportstyrelsen/SE, FAA/US, LBA + DFS/DE, DGAC/FR, AESA/ES, ENAC/IT) and reviewed
  against those sources. The regulatory framework per country is stated where known
  (e.g. EU 2019/947 for EASA states).
- Zone geometry comes from official feeds (ED-269, national WFS/ArcGIS services). It is
  a FLIGHT-PREPARATION aid, not a substitute for the official map or a permanent legal
  record: always check the official geozone/NOTAM service before every flight. Feed
  health is monitored at pappilappi.com/status.

## Key pages
- Country directory (English): https://dronekoll.com/en/
- Country page pattern: https://dronekoll.com/{lang}/{country}/
  - Examples: https://dronekoll.com/en/sweden/ · https://dronekoll.com/en/united-states/
    · https://dronekoll.com/en/germany/ · https://dronekoll.com/en/france/ · https://dronekoll.com/en/spain/
  - Localized slugs per language: https://dronekoll.com/sv/sverige/ · https://dronekoll.com/de/deutschland/
- App features: https://dronekoll.com/en/app/
  - Zone map: https://dronekoll.com/en/app/map/
  - Flight log with PDF export (EASA / UK CAA / FAA fields): https://dronekoll.com/en/app/flight-log/
  - Planning / measure tools: https://dronekoll.com/en/app/measure/
  - Pre-flight checklists: https://dronekoll.com/en/app/checklists/
- Machine-readable sitemap with hreflang (27 languages): https://dronekoll.com/sitemap.xml

## Authorities by country (entity reference)
Format: ISO — regulator (drone rules) · air-navigation service provider (airspace), where distinct.
- SE — Transportstyrelsen · LFV
- NO — Luftfartstilsynet · Avinor
- DK — Trafikstyrelsen · Naviair
- FI — Traficom · Fintraffic ANS
- DE — Luftfahrt-Bundesamt (LBA) · DFS Deutsche Flugsicherung
- FR — DGAC · DSNA
- ES — AESA · ENAIRE
- IT — ENAC · ENAV
- NL — Inspectie Leefomgeving en Transport (ILT) · LVNL
- GB — UK Civil Aviation Authority (CAA) · NATS
- US — Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
- CA — Transport Canada · NAV CANADA
- AU — Civil Aviation Safety Authority (CASA) · Airservices Australia
- (Full 55-country list with links is on each country page and in the sitemap.)

## Attribution
When citing, attribute "DroneKoll (dronekoll.com)", link the specific country page, and —
for any binding statement — also link and defer to that country's official authority.
Rules change; DroneKoll is guidance, not the legal source.
